prompt: Wide architectural eye-level view of the same Andover MA 1980s colonial dining room AFTER renovation. The full coffered ceiling grid is now visible across the frame — white-painted maple millwork forming six clean recessed coffer boxes with stacked crown and panel-mold profiles, warm-stained red oak beams dividing the grid. The chandelier is now a simple matte-black drum fixture. Walls repainted soft warm white. A long oak farm-table with two woven cane chairs sits below. Refinished wide-plank pine floor underneath. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens, late afternoon sun rakes through a side window casting warm slanting bars across the table top and up onto the coffers. A red Anjo-style ceramic mug sits on the table as the only fully saturated color. High-contrast slightly desaturated palette, working-contractor catalog feel, NOT HGTV bright, NOT luxury staged. No readable text, no logos.
